title: strychnos acuta
intro: Strychnos acuta, also known as the Poison Nut, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and West Africa, characterized by its large, yellowish-green fruit and edible seeds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimalarial, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects. The seeds of Strychnos acuta have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the seeds are typically dried, powdered, and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although caution is advised due to the plant's toxic nature.
